content:
Reading through the github readme, I see that the "**existing Term Base and Style Guide**" (https://drive.google.com/drive/u/0/folders/1NVBQGdhmr6w6tx9RzpX-BDKxdQD-MX9m) isn't available to everyone with the link.

I understand you might need some way to control access, however it's gonna be a hassle to approve individually everyone, so I suggest either opening it or having it as a resource in discord.

embeds.title: Google Drive: Sign-in
embeds.description: Access Google Drive with a Google account (for personal use) or Google Workspace account (for business use).

content:
Same policies are applied to the source file though for those I understand that the need for control is higher.
*Still, a way to identify oneself using something else than a google account would be appreciated, but that's not essential.*
To be clear : my opinion is that any document that is not meant to be modified by volunteers should not require an additional verification and should be accessible from this server.

1. currently the \@everyone role has the right to make invites (i.e I was able to make this : https://discord.gg/tfbmx53f), which I suppose is not something you want.
2. <#1402537664619479102> is fine, but that could be done in a slightly less clunky way with Discord's onboarding system (as done in the main server). *Note that currently all language channels are visible regardless of chosen language roles, which seems unintended*.
3. to chat/interact in the server one must accept the rules (and that's a good thing !), however it looks like you left in the default rules, maybe a reminder to not share the server/respect the NDA would be nice, especially since there is nothing preventing me from coming here before signing it.
4. the category names being in chinese is slightly confusing, especially for a project mainly aimed at english-speaking volunteers 🤓☝️. 
5. the automatic welcome message fall in the <#1402537664619479105> channel for some reason, you can disable them or push them to a more relevant channel in **Server Settings** > **Engagement**
6. the default notifications are set to "*all messages*", please don't, set them to "*\@mentions only*".
7. You can/should probably make this server an "apply to join" server in order to have a finer control over who can come in (and maybe only let people in once they've signed the NDA).
See below for the "apply to join" server type (found in **Server Settings** > **Access**) :

Addendum to **2.** the order of the emotes on the role selection message is wrong (tester-proofreader-translator in text, translator-proofreader-tester in emotes).
Also that's not very colorblind friendly 🤓☝️ since the two missplaced emotes have very similar colors
